This family is part of the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants that are often found in tropical and subtropical regions. The order Magnoliales is further classified under the class Magnoliopsida, which is one of the two main classes of flowering plants. Overall, Uvariella leptopoda's classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history as a flowering plant.

intro: Uvariella leptopoda, commonly known as the "Snake Palm," is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Annonaceae. Traditionally, Uvariella leptopoda is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties.
